Two Israeli NGOs warn of a 'de facto annexation at an unprecedented pace' in the West Bank
subhed: Israel now controls 18% of the occupied territories, compared to 7% before the war in Gaza. A report from the non-profit organizations Peace Now and Kerem Navot has been documenting the acceleration of land grabs, the construction of settler-only roads and the expulsion of local communities over the past three and a half years.

title: Macron expresses 'belief that a new era is dawning in Syria' in Damascus visit
subhed: The French president became the first European head of state to visit Damascus since the fall of Bashar al-Assad. Paris is seeking to retain influence in a country central to the Middle East's political and economic future.

title: Albert Camus archives, including manuscripts, forged documents and letters, join national collection
subhed: The archives, previously kept within the writer's family, has been acquired by France's national library for €9 million with support from Hermès and CIC.

title: Pyrénées wildfires destroy everything in their path: 'I didn't think I'd make it out alive'
subhed: The residents of Ille-sur-Têt, a town that was evacuated, remain in shock as the Trévillach wildfire – the largest the region has seen in 50 years – was still not under control on Monday evening.

title: Philosopher Mélanie Plouviez: 'The rich are not rich alone; they are in a social world that protects them'
subhed: Wealth is not determined solely by the possessions and assets one accumulates, philosopher Mélanie Plouviez explains in an interview with Le Monde. Since antiquity, a person's fortune has also been defined by one's social standing, relationships of dominance, dependence and honor.
